questions.q: In which year was the Planning Commission of India set up?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was established in March 1950 by a resolution of the Government of India.

questions.q: What were the planning documents formulated by the Planning Commission popularly called?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was best known for formulating India's Five-Year Plans for economic and social development.

questions.q: In which year was the Planning Commission replaced by a new body?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was wound up in 2014; its successor, NITI Aayog, was formally established on 1 January 2015.

questions.q: Which body replaced the Planning Commission?
questions.explanation: NITI Aayog was established in 2015 as the successor institution to the Planning Commission.

questions.q: Who served as the ex-officio Chairman of the Planning Commission?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister of India served as the ex-officio Chairman of the Planning Commission.

questions.q: What was the primary focus of the Planning Commission's work?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission guided India's economic and social development through its plans.

questions.q: How many years did each standard Five-Year Plan cover?
questions.explanation: As the name indicates, each Five-Year Plan covered a period of five years.

questions.q: The Planning Commission was created by which type of instrument?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was set up by an executive resolution of the Government of India, not by the Constitution or a statute.

questions.q: In which city was the Planning Commission headquartered?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was headquartered at Yojana Bhavan in New Delhi.

questions.q: Who was the first Prime Minister of India during whose tenure the Planning Commission was created?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was set up in 1950 during the tenure of Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru, who chaired it.

questions.q: The First Five-Year Plan, launched by the Planning Commission, began in which year?
questions.explanation: The First Five-Year Plan covered the period 1951 to 1956.

questions.q: The First Five-Year Plan placed primary emphasis on which sector?
questions.explanation: The First Five-Year Plan (1951-56) focused mainly on agriculture, irrigation and rehabilitation.

questions.q: The Second Five-Year Plan is associated with a development model named after which statistician?
questions.explanation: The Second Five-Year Plan (1956-61) was based on the Mahalanobis model, emphasising heavy industry.

questions.q: What was the official designation of the deputy head who handled the day-to-day functioning of the Planning Commission?
questions.explanation: The Deputy Chairman, often called the Vice-Chairman, held cabinet rank and managed the Commission's working.

questions.q: During a period in the 1960s, planning was briefly interrupted and replaced by Annual Plans. These were known as the:
questions.explanation: Between 1966 and 1969 there was a 'Plan holiday' during which three Annual Plans replaced a Five-Year Plan.

questions.q: Which body, presided over by the Prime Minister, was responsible for approving the Five-Year Plans prepared by the Planning Commission?
questions.explanation: The National Development Council, with the Prime Minister and Chief Ministers, gave final approval to the plans.

questions.q: The Planning Commission was an example of which kind of body in Indian governance?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was neither constitutional nor statutory; it was created by an executive resolution.

questions.q: Which Five-Year Plan was the last to be formulated by the Planning Commission before its replacement?
questions.explanation: The Twelfth Five-Year Plan (2012-17) was the last formulated under the Planning Commission.

questions.q: The model of planning followed in India under the Planning Commission was broadly described as which type?
questions.explanation: India followed a mixed-economy approach, combining state planning with a private sector.

questions.q: The idea of a planning body in India was influenced by an earlier committee set up by the Indian National Congress in 1938 called the:
questions.explanation: The National Planning Committee of 1938, chaired by Jawaharlal Nehru, was a precursor to the idea of central planning.

questions.q: One key function of the Planning Commission was the allocation of central plan assistance to which entities?
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission played a major role in allocating plan funds to the states and union territories.

questions.q: Which monthly journal was associated with the Planning Commission for disseminating plan information?
questions.explanation: The journal Yojana, named after the Hindi word for plan, was associated with development planning.

questions.q: Who was the first Deputy Chairman of the Planning Commission?
questions.explanation: Gulzarilal Nanda served as the first Deputy Chairman of the Planning Commission.

questions.q: The Third Five-Year Plan (1961-66) was significantly affected by which external events?
questions.explanation: The Third Plan was disrupted by the 1962 and 1965 wars and by severe droughts.

questions.q: Which Five-Year Plan introduced the influential 'Garibi Hatao' (Remove Poverty) emphasis?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-79) emphasised poverty removal and self-reliance.

questions.q: The Planning Commission introduced a measure to identify the poor known as the:
questions.explanation: The Planning Commission was responsible for estimating the official Poverty Line and poverty ratios in India.

questions.q: The concept of 'rolling plans' as an alternative to fixed Five-Year Plans was advocated by which economist associated with India?
questions.explanation: Gunnar Myrdal advocated the concept of rolling plans, which India briefly adopted in the late 1970s.

questions.q: Approximately how many Five-Year Plans were formulated in total during the existence of the Planning Commission?
questions.explanation: Twelve Five-Year Plans were formulated, from the First (1951-56) to the Twelfth (2012-17).

questions.q: The Planning Commission's allocations were guided by an internal body of experts known as the:
questions.explanation: The Programme Evaluation Organisation within the Planning Commission assessed the implementation of development programmes.

questions.q: On which date in 1950 was the resolution establishing the Planning Commission adopted?
questions.explanation: The Government of India resolution establishing the Planning Commission was adopted on 15 March 1950.
